description: Wonderful experience in terms of project management, visual roadmap.It makes team collaboration, brainstorming, and meetings more interactive and organized. The interface is user-friendly, and real-time editing keeps everyone aligned. Overall, it’s a reliable and efficient tool for visual project management.
author: Shrishail K.
pros: Miro offers strong value for money, especially for teams needing a flexible collaborative whiteboard, though larger plans can be pricey. Customer support is responsive with solid documentation, but live help can be slow at peak times. The platform scores high on ease of use, with intuitive drag-and-drop tools and templates that reduce the learning curve. Organizational management features like team spaces and permissions help keep boards structured, while meetings feel more engaging with real-time co-editing and presentation modes. Workflow management integrates well with tools like Jira and Asana, making task tracking smoother. Overall navigation is clean and logical, though very large boards can occasionally feel cluttered.
cons: It gives the ready made templates for workflows, inbuilt AI features, and endless whiteboard. important is value for money.

description: Miro has genuinely changed how our team collaborates, especially working remotely. Before, aligning everyone on ideas or plans meant long email threads or meetings that could've been a document. Now we just open a board and figure things out together in real time. It became one of those tools that you don't realize how much you rely on until you try to work without it. Planning sessions, brainstorming, feedback rounds — it all just lives in Miro now and it makes everything feel a lot less scattered. It's not perfect, but it fits naturally into the way modern teams actually work, and that's honestly what matters most.
author: saidi B.
pros: Honestly, Miro just feels like the kind of tool that gets out of your way and lets you think. The canvas is huge, everything is drag and drop, and you never really feel boxed in by the structure of it. What I liked most though is how easy it is to get everyone involved — even people who aren't particularly "visual" types. You send someone a link, they jump in, and within two minutes they're adding sticky notes and moving things around like they've been using it for years. There's almost no learning curve, which is rare for a collaboration tool. The templates are also genuinely useful. Not just filler actual ready-to-go boards for things like retrospectives, brainstorming sessions, or roadmap planning that you can just pick up and run with. It makes remote collaboration feel less like a compromise and more like you're actually in the same room together.
cons: The one thing that got to me after a while is how heavy it can feel once your boards start filling up. Like, the more content you add, the slower things get especially if you're on a video call at the same time. It's not a dealbreaker but you do notice it. Also, the pricing. It's generous enough to get started, but the moment your team grows or you need some of the more advanced features, you're looking at a jump in cost that feels a bit steep. Feels like some things that should just be included are locked behind higher tiers. And honestly? Sometimes the amount of options and templates can be a little overwhelming. You open it up to do something simple and suddenly you're spending 20 minutes just picking a board layout. First world problem, I know but still. Nothing that made me want to stop using it, just small friction points that add up over time.

review_title: Miro is a great way to visualize
description: Miro has been great for visualizing things for our customers. We have had some really great meetings due to Miro. When looking at this from a forward looking perspective though, Miro becomes a challenge to data security and data retention.
author: Allen F.
pros: Miro can be an awesome tool for visualizing ideas and organizing thoughts. The features and plugins are great and the support from the customer support team has been great as well.
cons: Once you're in Miro, your data is kind of trapped in the application. There are ways to export but there are size limits on exporting. What makes it more difficult is that there is no way to track the overall size of your board while you're designing it so when it comes time to export, I find myself breaking things into many smaller boards or frames. If we knew this before trying Miro, we probably would have looked into alternatives. Now we feel stuck.

review_title: Easy to use whiteboarding and charting tool with some administrative limitations
description: Miro has been a solid tool overall. It’s easy to use, makes it simple to visualise ideas using templates and drag-and-drop, and it’s great for sharing and collaborating. We’ve used it effectively to scope a CRM build and to map out requirements in a way that’s easy for others to understand. The main downsides are that structured diagrams don’t export in a useful hierarchy, overlapping items can be painful to work with because of the layers, and it would be better if you could add more detail to items without cluttering the board.
author: Matt B.
pros: It is very easy to use. The click and drag interface and the templated actions make visualising data and concepts easy. It is also easy to share and collaborate with others. The free licence tier makes it easy for others to join in.
cons: Though the data in flowcharts and mind maps are in a structured hierarchy, Miro does not export the data in that structure. Especially for csv the data comes out in one column and in the order in which it was added to Miro, which is illogical. Items on the board that cross each other or overlap are difficult to work with as the item you want might be buried behind other items. Miro operates its layers like Powerpoint, which is equally difficult to use. I find myself sending foreground items to the back multiple times to get to the item I want. Miro is only two dimensional. In my mind maps I want to be able to provide more information about an item in the map but the only way to do that is to create another label or postit note. I would love to be able to provide depth to an item where someone could hover and read a description of that item. Also, from an administrators perspective Miro only makes content created by my organisation visible to me in the enterprise tier, which is expensive. I cannot see what my staff are doing in Miro, therefore handing over from one employee to another is very difficult.

description: It’s a very good tools for collaboration and visual thinking, especially for cross-functional work where you need quick alignment and shared context. While large boards can become messy or slower over time, it’s still extremely effective when used with good structure and a clear purpose.
author: Rakhi V.
pros: It is how quickly it helps teams get aligned—whether it’s brainstorming, mapping flows, or running a workshop, everyone can contribute in real time without needing to be in the same room. I also really like how flexible it is: sticky notes, diagrams, templates, and frames make it easy to turn messy ideas into something structured and shareable.
cons: As boards get larger, Miro can start to feel a bit heavy—performance can slow down, and it becomes harder to find things unless the board is kept tidy. Also, when multiple people are editing at once, boards can get cluttered quickly, so it takes discipline (or strong templates) to keep things organized and easy to follow

review_title: The fun yet professional organizational tool
description: I’d describe my overall experience as good. It does have it’s disadvantages, such as the limit on dashboards and the small number of free templates, but it also has many advantages that make virtual teamwork easier and faster. Once you get the hang of it, it becomes a very useful and even fun tool!
author: Orest R.
pros: What I most enjoyed of Miro is that it turned out really fun and easy to use for making mental maps, flowcharts and diagrams. It helps automatizing the creation of these tools, because it’s a big dashboard without space limits, and you just need to add the elements you want without worrying about the space limit (if it’s going to be too small or too big). It’s also very collaborative, which is something’s that stands out when you got to work with groups of coworkers; it has been a game changer for me and my peers when it comes to teamwork and organizing our works and projects. It feels fun, chill, but professional at the same time. The navigation in the platform is fast and intuitive; you don’t need to type much to find what you want. Talking about the elements, I was thrilled when I saw the variety of cool things (Colorful post-its, arrows, pie charts, bar charts, etc) you can add to make the dashboard more organized and easier to digest. Loved that!
cons: For the cons, the one that stood out the most for me, is that the free version doesn’t let me create more than 3 dashboards at the same time, because when I need to open many dashboards for the big projects, then I need to use another tool or just try to adjust to the limit it is giving me, and it’s not really comfortable.

description: I use Miro regularly for workshops, trainings, and brainstorming sessions with clients, and it's become hard to imagine running those formats without it. The flexibility, the depth of features, and the fact that most people already have it in their toolkit makes it a reliable go-to. It's one of the few tools where the visual format actually adds value rather than just adding steps.
author: Said S.
pros: Miro fits naturally into how I work as a freelancer. The visual whiteboard is flexible enough to handle anything from structured workshop flows to open brainstorming sessions. Most clients already know it, which saves a lot of onboarding time. The feature set is deep without feeling overwhelming once you're in it.
cons: The free plan is limited - you'll likely need a paid tier to get the most out of it for client work.

description: I really like it, found it really easy to get started and loved how I could customise the look of it all.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Its very good UX, very visually pleasing adn very easy to get started, use and share with others. It's flexible and makes brainstromign easy, interactive and inclusive.
cons: You have to pay for a lot of the most useful features, which is frustrating. you can only have a small number of boards on the free version.

review_title: A reliable and flexible tool for visual collaboration and team brainstorming
description: Overall, my experience with Miro has been smooth and productive, especially for team collaboration, brainstorming sessions, and organizing workflows visually.
author: Taiba B.
pros: Miro makes real-time collaboration very easy with its intuitive interface, flexible canvas, and a wide range of templates that help speed up brainstorming and planning.
cons: Large boards can become cluttered and slightly hard to navigate, and performance may slow down when working with complex or content-heavy boards.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Take Charge of Project Statuses with Miro!
description: I enjoyed using Miro for project management because it allowed me to visually collaborate with other users and absorb information while getting stuff done together.
author: Nicole G.
pros: Miro was easy to use with multiple collaborators - I could see everything we were working on in the same frame, and I could zoom in and out to focus on specific parts.
cons: It could get laggy with lots of users on the file, sometimes we would have trouble locating specific parts of the project because there were too many details in one screen.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Exploring the Benefits of Innovative Collaboration Tool .
description: Overall Miro has been an awesome tool that enabled seamless and effective collaboration in our daily lives.
author: Brock G.
pros: Amazed the way it has very friendly and intuitive interface making it easy to use.I like Miro ease of creating and sharing diagrams and other visual tools that allow us to clearly visualize and collaborate on our projects .Offer endless canvas that bring our ideas and projects into life.Provide flexible templates that saves time on task management. Also offer powerful and intuitive Agile tools .
cons: Dashboard in Miro needs some improvement, other than this it's an outstanding platform with lots of features.

description: My team thrives on visual collaboration and Miro is fantastic for brainstorming and strategy sessions.
author: Angela O.
pros: The ease of use, voting, and infinite canvas simplifies my idea mapping and brainstorming sessions. It’s the best tool I have user for creating design diagrams.
cons: I did not find any drawback using this tool, there is a lot of room to set up many boards, workspaces or tabs.

review_title: Absolutely brilliant and no going back!
description: A wonderful contribution to the world of data and idea visualisation. Easy and accesible for all ages and types of people. Made easier with a desktop and mouse, but equally at home on an iPad. You can spend hours expanding your visualisations with a smile on your face. A joy to use and so useful for teaching, learning and other project visualisation applications
author: Will B.
pros: I absolutely love Miro. It has helped me with teaching tasks, presentation tasks, group collaboration and simply visualising ideas and structures. It is incredibly well designed and thought through. It looks beautiful and is a joy to use. They have very appealing licenses for Universities which makes getting everyone onboard a breeze. Really reccomend for a wide range of uses.
cons: It is hard to find fault with Miro, but it might simply be that it takes a tiny bit of time and commitment to learn the ropes and become a creative genius. The time is well spent though, as once you master the art of creating engaging designs and presentations it is hard to imagine going back to anything else.

description: Overall my experience is ok. I like to use it when I want to make a cisual coillaboration with the team.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like about Miro, the easy way to contribute with other people at the same time about a project and the usefull tools that they provide.
cons: What I don't like about Miro is that the mobile side doesn´t work well nd crush really easily. Because sometimes the whiteboard is too big, there is extra downloading that makes things hard.

author: Koichi M.
pros: Miro includes features like a built-in timer, an anonymous voting system, and a "Bring to me" function to force all participants to follow what you are presenting.
cons: For a beginner, the interface can be intimidating. There are so many tools and menus that it's easy to feel lost during your first few sessions.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We use this for our agile/scrum stand-up meetings as well as other meetings we hold offline for task management.
cons: Did not encounter any issues, we really like the Miro integration with other tools including Teams and Slack.

description: Use of Miro software has made our meetings become more productive and interactive by providing quality videos and ensuring unlimited visual workspaces.
author: Mmaestri G.
pros: Miro software integration with meeting solutions and availability of a role based access. Real-time collaboration capabilities of the software and existence of unlimited screen sharing features.
cons: The product is very standard. Customer services providers of the tool are responsive and helpful. No aversion

review_title: A good tool for ideation and collaboration.
description: Miro is affordable and suits businesses and organisations of sizes. Customer services of the product are professionals and available 24/7. My experience with Miro has been great.
author: Boualeme H.
pros: Ability of the software in boosting productivity and ensuring exceptional vitual collaboration experience.
cons: So far so good. The product has served us well. It is a well designed collaboration software no dislike.

review_title: Decent diagramming, missing some features, better than Visio
description: Overall, good diagramming software. I wish AWS icons/templates were already included. The integration with Jira is good. Price is okay with the starter plan.
author: Monica G.
pros: I like the diagramming/whiteboarding functionality. I like being able to share the board. Basic flowchart is good. I like the emphasis on collaboration. Works with Jira.
cons: I wish the AWS templates were included. It is a pain to upgrade plans for some AWS icons. Lucidchart is better for this part. The fish bone template needs improvement. I wish I could embed timelines into slide decks/presentations. I wish I could use "tab" to add to the mind map.

author: Victoria S.
pros: I made hybrid team meetings using Miro and incorporating face-to-face and online participants. The voting and Talktrack recording facilitated participation, follow-ups and ensured direct action items reached the right channel.
cons: The video call option in Miro was not constantly secure in audio/video. Sometimes calls used to drop or were as low as a whisper.

review_title: Best tool for bringing ideas together
description: Miro provides me easy tools for creating product ideas comparing prototype with endless panning. It's like having a big whiteboard the size of a room where you can keep ideating all your scattered ideas.
author: Babatunde O.
pros: Miro is one of the best product ideation tool i have ever used with my team. The endless screen extension just works perfectly on wider screens
cons: I would speak specifically on the limited seat available for the free version of the team plan. I once once a paid users and lost access to my files after membership expiration which was not cool

review_title: Very nice software to work in group!
description: I really appreciate to work with Mirro. The sync work and the boards with simultaneous group work makes the life very easy. As we develop many projects with different teams and different companies, the mirror helps a lot to deal with the brain storms, development projects until final conclusion.
author: Joao gabriel C.
pros: It is so interesting and useful software to work in group in presence or online. The features are very effective and interesting.
cons: No critics about it, I am happy with the software
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Miro is great! I have used a few virtual whiteboard tools and Miro is definitely the best in terms of features, ease of collaboration and templates.
author: Heather K.
pros: It is really intuitive to use and has so many great features. The miroverse is amazing for finding templates for retros, and other collaboration boards created by the community
cons: It is quite an expensive tool to use. As a company we have not been able to afford to have everyone in the company on a contributors licence which has been difficult

description: Miro is my favorite tool that I have used when collaborating with my team. I use this tool primarily for flow chart creation to demonstrate user flow and technical architecture diagrams to my clients. My clients are always impressed when I bring out the miro documentation!
author: Jon F.
pros: Easy to use, great collaboration tool, and awesome pre-made templates
cons: Miro doesn't have many flaws that I can think of to be honest. It's my favorite, most used tool.

description: The best whiteboarding tool with a wide variety of templates to choose from which can be used for collaboration with multiple users .There are proper security /access controls such as password protection and view/edit access controls which can be individually customised as per the user to prevent unauthorised editing of the board.Overall I highly recommend the tool
author: Issac P.
pros: Its a great whiteboard tool with an unlimited canvas. Supports a variety of predefined templates to choose from for sprint planning,brainstorming etc
cons: No suits my use case I did not find any issue with miro yet
